verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 (Cysteine-Rich Secretory Protein 1) is a secreted protein of the CRISP family produced primarily in the epididymis. It is secreted into the epididymal lumen and associates with the sperm surface during epididymal transit, where it binds to the sperm plasma membrane in a wash-resistant manner to modulate sperm function (zona pellucida binding, ion channel regulation). This stable post-secretory association with the sperm cell surface qualifies as contextual surface accessibility (tissue-restricted to reproductive/germline cells, stable surface attachment on sperm). Outside of male reproductive biology, the protein is essentially secreted-only.
